claim: GPT-4o mini is 30x cheaper than GPT-4 and 20x cheaper than Claude 3.5 Sonnet while maintaining only 3-13% accuracy drop across benchmarks
actionable: Test GPT-4o mini on your tasks before paying for larger models; you may get acceptable results at 20-30x lower cost

claim: Fusion chains: run the same prompts across multiple cheap models (e.g., 4 GPT-4o mini runs), merge outputs with an evaluator function to match SOTA quality
actionable: For cost-sensitive tasks: run cheap models in parallel, merge their outputs with a structured evaluator; you get SOTA quality at 20% cost

claim: Sequential prompt chains with step-by-step reasoning amplify cheap model performance; the 2-3% accuracy loss from cheaper models is recovered through better prompting
